comparisons.description: This could fund a $60,000 minimum salary for every public school teacher in America
This could cover the cost of renewing all ACA enhanced premium tax credits cut by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBA)
comparisons.sourceText: Offsetting ACA Enhanced Subsidy Extensions | Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

comparisons.description: This could cap childcare costs at 7% of household income for every American family for one year
comparisons.sourceText: Early Learning and Care in the Build Back Better Act, Congressional Budget Office

comparisons.description: This could fund spending for the entire fiscal year 2024 Department of Homeland Security Budget
comparisons.sourceText: U.S. Department of Homeland Security FY 2024 Budget, U.S. Department of the Treasury

comparisons.description: This could fund spending for the entire fiscal year 2024 Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) and the Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) program
comparisons.sourceText: SNAP and WIC FY 2024 Spending, USDA Economic Research Service

comparisons.description: This could eliminate federal income taxes entirely for every individual earning up to $46,000/year and every married couple earning up to $92,000/year
comparisons.sourceText: IRS Statistics of Income — Individual Income Tax Returns (Publication 1304)

comparisons.description: This could fund spending for the entire fiscal year 2024 Department of Education budget
comparisons.sourceText: U.S. Department of Education FY 2024 Budget, U.S. Department of the Treasury

comparisons.description: This could fund spending for the entire fiscal year 2024 Department of Veterans Affairs
comparisons.sourceText: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs FY 2024 Budget, U.S. Department of the Treasury

comparisons.description: This could fund a significant portion of federal Medicare spending
comparisons.sourceText: CBO Medicare Spending FY 2024, Congressional Budget Office

comparisons.description: This covers spending for an entire year for the federal portion of Medicaid payments
comparisons.sourceText: MACStats: Medicaid and CHIP Data Book February 2026

comparisons.description: This covers spending for an entire year of the federal portion of Medicaid spending and the entire CHIP program
comparisons.sourceText: MACStats: Medicaid and CHIP Data Book February 2026

comparisons.description: This could fully eliminate the employer-side payroll tax on wages for one year, cutting employment costs for every business and boosting take-home pay for every worker
comparisons.sourceText: What Kinds of Revenue Does the Government Collect?, Bipartisan Policy Center

comparisons.description: This could fund a one-time $3,000 direct payment to every individual — $12,000 for a family of four — in households earning $150,000 or less
comparisons.sourceText: NEWS: Sanders and Khanna Introduce Legislation to Tax Billionaire Wealth and Invest in Working Families

comparisons.description: This approaches the total annual cost of running the U.S. federal government
comparisons.sourceText: U.S. Department of the Treasury FY 2024 Federal Spending
